extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won a crash-marred sprint finish in Salerno after mid-30s km/h average speed neutered team tactics and attrition removed key sprinters at 7km.
tactical_patterns: Average speed mid-30s km/h in wet conditions eliminated organized leadouts, turning finale into survival-based positioning lottery.
Crash at 7km removed Ackermann/Gaviria but Roglič/Leknessund demonstrated superior chase/positioning to regain contact before 3km rule.
Alpecin/Trek/DSM attempted leadout organization but wet roads favored lone sprinters over coordinated trains in final 3km.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: crashed twice (dog incident early, separate crash sub-3km) suggesting poor wet-road positioning awareness despite no time loss
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: showed exceptional chase skills recovering from 7km crash to regain peloton, GC-caliber bike handling in chaos
rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: positive: navigated both major crashes (7km and finale) without losing maglia rosa, superior wet positioning for non-specialist
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: back wheel slipped on white line in final 300m, age/handling limitations evident in technical wet sprint
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: neutral: took KOM points then abandoned breakaway effort, treating stage as training ride rather than contesting
